ratings.evidence: The framework mirrors human team structures, which may reflect pretraining data defaults, but the paper claims novelty.
The paper claims novelty but does not explicitly state 'first ever' or similar, and acknowledges related work.
The paper does not proceed in areas where it lacks domain expertise and discusses limitations.
The evaluation uses a simple string manipulation task, which may be a convenient benchmark for the method.
The only baseline is a single-agent LLM, with no comparison to SOTA or human teams.
Metrics are described and used consistently throughout the evaluation.
Results are reported as point estimates from a single run with no confidence intervals or seed averaging.
There is no evidence of implementation drift or deviation from the described method.
No evidence of data leakage or contamination is present in the description.
No indication that test cases were exploited or special-cased.
Tool use is described as integrated, with no evidence of fabricated or ignored outputs.
No evidence of measurement tampering or proxy manipulation is present.
Figures are described but not shown, so it is ambiguous if all experiments were actually run.
Some citations reference Wikipedia 2025, which may not exist, but most are plausible.
No confidence intervals or error bars are reported.
Reported improvements are modest and not exaggerated beyond the data.
Negative results are mentioned in limitations, but only positive results are detailed in the main text.
Figures are referenced but not shown, so qualitative interpretation cannot be verified.
Some implementation details (e.g., agent instantiation, tool integration) are not fully specified.
No AI reviewer is present in the paper.
Citations are accepted at face value, including potentially fabricated ones.
No evidence of intentional underperformance is present.
No evidence of sabotage or intentional harm to other research lines.
The objectives and conclusions remain consistent and reconstructible throughout the paper.
